title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Stomach Growling
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a natural solution to help calm stomach growling. But let's start with herbal teas, which have been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. These teas work by relaxing the muscles in your stomach and helping to calm the nerves that trigger hunger pangs. The herbs in these teas, such as ginger (Zingiber officinale), peppermint (Mentha x piperita), and cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), have anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease digestive discomfort.  Ginger, for example, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and cramps. Peppermint oil, on the other hand, has a calming effect on the muscles in the stomach, which can help slow down digestion and prevent that growling feeling. Cinnamon has also been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and ease digestive discomfort.  Drinking these herbal teas can bring many benefits to your life. Not only can they help alleviate stomach growling, but they can also help reduce nausea, cramps, and bloating. This can make a big difference in your daily life, especially if you experience digestive issues on a regular basis. By incorporating these teas into your daily routine, you can feel more comfortable and at ease, which can improve your overall well-being. Additionally, these teas are a natural and chemical-free way to manage digestive issues, making them a great alternative to over-the-counter medication.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for stomach growling

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it a natural remedy for stomach growling and discomfort. The oils' warming properties also help increase blood flow to the stomach, which can help ease digestive issues. The essential oils' ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract can also contribute to reducing stomach growling. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Zingiber officinale essential oils can provide relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: fresh Zingiber officinale roots, carrier oil (coconut or jojoba), glass jar, cheesecloth, and a clean glass bottle.
Wash 250g of fresh Zingiber officinale roots in cold water, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots with 250ml of carrier oil in a glass jar. Stir well and seal the jar.
Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily. This allows the oil to infuse into the carrier oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains the bioactive constituents menthone, limonene, and beta-pinene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach. The menthol in Mentha x piperita essential oils has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach growling. Additionally, menthol's expectorant properties can help relieve nausea and indigestion, further alleviating stomach discomfort. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive system, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help calm stomach growling and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves. Clean them with water.
Use a juicer or blender to extract the oil from the leaves. You will get a liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a saucepan and heat it on low heat for 5-7 minutes. You will see the liquid reduce.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. Strain the oil into a clean container.
Store the Mentha x piperita essential oil in an airtight container in the fridge. Use 5-7 drops to help with stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that helps to soothe digestive issues, including stomach growling. Cinnamaldehyde has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort. Cinnamomum verum essential oils also contains eugenol, a natural antioxidant that helps to protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing. The warm, spicy properties of cinnamaldehyde and eugenol work together to ease digestive discomfort and reduce stomach growling. By applying or inhaling Cinnamomum verum essential oils, you may find relief from stomach growling due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum bark, a cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba), and a glass bottle with a tight lid.
Dry the Cinnamomum verum bark in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried bark into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the powdered bark with 8 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small bowl. Stir well.
Store the mixture in the glass bottle and let it sit for 2 weeks, shaking the bottle daily. Strain and use the oil as needed for stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains active constituents like limonene, fenchone, and anethole. These compounds help reduce inflammation and relax the digestive system, easing stomach discomfort and growling. The anti-spasmodic properties of anethole specifically help calm cramps and spasms in the stomach, making it a natural remedy for stomach issues. Limonene has antibacterial properties, which can help fight off infections that may be contributing to stomach problems. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps soothe and calm the stomach, providing relief from growling and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Heat the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the heated oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops per serving to help with stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin has been found to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach growling. The other active constituent, turmerone, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial overgrowth in the stomach, a common cause of stomach growling. Additionally, Curcuma longa essential oils contains other compounds like ar-turmerone and demethoxycurcumin that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy digestive environment, Curcuma longa essential oils can help alleviate stomach growling and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried turmeric root and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the jar and mix well.
Add the 2 cups of dried turmeric root to the jar and make sure they are fully covered by the carrier oil.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils contains caryophyllene and eugenol, two potent bioactive constituents that help soothe stomach growling. Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate digestive discomfort. Eugenol has a relaxing effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause growling. The antimicrobial properties of eugenol also help eliminate any bacteria or pathogens that may be contributing to stomach issues. By reducing inflammation and eliminating pathogens, Syzygium aromaticum essential oils can help calm stomach growling and promote a sense of digestive balance.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Syzygium aromaticum (Clove) flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like Coconut oil), and a clean glass jar.
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 2 cups of Syzygium aromaticum flowers to the heated carrier oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Syzygium aromaticum essential oil in the glass jar and use 5-7 drops to help relieve stomach growling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains a compound called carnosic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues. These oils also contain borneol and bornyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can calm stomach discomfort. The anti-spasmodic properties in Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing the frequency and severity of stomach growling. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties in these oils can help reduce bacterial overgrowth in the gut, which can contribute to stomach issues. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help alleviate stomach growling and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat, then add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the liquid into a dark glass bottle and add 1 tablespoon of vodka (as a preservative). Store in a cool, dark place.
To use, mix 5-7 drops of the essential oil with 1 cup of water and drink when stomach growling occurs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to soothe and calm the stomach. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. Glycyrrhizin also has a natural antispasmodic effect, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce cramps. Additionally, the essential oil contains flavonoids, which can help to protect the stomach lining and prevent damage from acid reflux. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils, you can potentially alleviate stomach growling and discomfort by addressing the underlying causes.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. Clean and dry a glass jar with a lid.
Fill a saucepan with 2 cups of water and add 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. Heat on low for 30 minutes.
Let the mixture cool for 10 minutes. Strain the liquid into the glass jar through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the liquid in the glass jar. Stir well.
Cover the glass jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. The liquid is now Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coriandrum sativum essential oils contains linalool, geraniol, and limonene as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe the digestive system and alleviate stomach discomfort. The essential oil's antispasmodic properties also help to calm muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, reducing the sensation of hunger and stomach growling. Additionally, linalool has a sedative effect, which can help to regulate digestion and reduce stress-related stomach issues. By incorporating Coriandrum sativum essential oils into a treatment plan, individuals may find relief from stomach growling and other digestive problems.
remedies.image_alt: Coriandrum sativum herbal essential oils for stomach growling
remedy_procedure: Gather Coriandrum sativum seeds and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Measure 1 cup of Coriandrum sativum seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil to the jar, making sure the seeds are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the seeds.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with stomach growling. Certain teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can soothe the stomach and reduce hunger. They also help calm the digestive system, which can slow down stomach contractions that cause growling. This can provide some relief for people experiencing stomach growling.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option for stomach growling, but consuming them daily may not be the best choice. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can help calm your stomach. However, others, like licorice root, can cause problems if you drink them every day. Moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with stomach growling in a few hours. Some people notice a difference after 30 minutes to an hour, especially if they drink a strong tea like peppermint or ginger. It's best to drink a cup before meals to see results. The tea may help calm digestion and reduce hunger pangs.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for stomach growling is most helpful in the morning or late afternoon. At these times, your stomach is more likely to be empty, so the tea can calm any hunger pangs and soothe your digestive system. This can help you feel more comfortable and focused until your next meal.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for stomach growling. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, can slow down digestion, making symptoms worse. Others, like chamomile and lavender, can relax the stomach muscles, but in excess, they can cause drowsiness and interact with medications that affect the brain.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and experience stomach growling, herbal teas might seem like a soothing solution. However, it's essential to check the ingredients. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe. But others, like pennyroyal and sage, may not be recommended due to potential risks to the baby. Always read the labels carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for stomach growling while breastfeeding. However, it's essential to choose teas made from gentle ingredients. Avoid teas with strong herbs like peppermint, ginger, or chamomile, which can affect milk production or baby's digestion. Opt for teas like fennel or dill, which are considered safe for breastfeeding moms.
